chunks.text: Richard J. Fateman is Professor Emeritus at UC Berkeley's EECS department. His contact information includes 441 Soda Hall, phone 510-847-2368, email fateman@cs.berkeley.edu, and office hours by appointment. He received a BS in Physics and Mathematics from Union College in 1966, and a Ph.D. in Applied Mathematics from Harvard University in 1971. His thesis work involved designing algorithms for the Macsyma computer algebra system.
chunks.question: What is Richard Fateman's academic background?
chunks.answer: BS from Union College, PhD from Harvard.

chunks.text: After teaching at MIT from 1971 to 1974, Fateman joined Berkeley's Computer Science faculty. He contributed to Berkeley's VAX UNIX system and led Franz Lisp development. He served on the Common Lisp design committee and IEEE 754 floating-point standards committee. From 1987 to 1990, he was Berkeley's Computer Science Chair. He has been principal investigator on grants from DOE, NSF, and industrial firms.
chunks.question: What key roles did Fateman have at Berkeley?
chunks.answer: Led Franz Lisp and served as CS Chair.

chunks.text: Fateman has authored over 75 technical papers on computer algebra, programming languages, scientific applications, and document image analysis. His research areas include Artificial Intelligence, Scientific Computing, computer algebra systems, programming environments, symbolic mathematical computation, and document image analysis with multimodal mathematics input.
chunks.question: What are Fateman's main research areas?
chunks.answer: Computer algebra, programming languages, document analysis.

chunks.text: Selected publications include: 'Software fault prevention by language choice: Why C is not my favorite language' (2002), 'Problem solving environments and symbolic computing' (2000), 'Symbolic computation of turbulence and energy dissipation in the Taylor vortex model' (1998), 'Optical character recognition and parsing of typeset mathematics' (1996), and 'Fast floating-point processing in Common Lisp' (1995).
chunks.question: What is a notable publication by Fateman?
chunks.answer: Software fault prevention by language choice.

chunks.text: Fateman received the honor of Association for Computing Machinery (ACM) Fellow in 1999. His work spans computer algebra systems, programming languages, and scientific computing, with significant contributions to academic and industrial projects through grants and publications.
chunks.question: What award did Fateman receive in 1999?
